title: rosa × andegavensis collina
intro: Rosa × andegavensis collina, also known as Andegavensis Collina, is a type of hybrid rose plant that is a cross between Rosa gallica and Rosa rubiginosa, resulting in a fragrant and delicate bloom with pink and white hues. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa × andegavensis collina include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed for its medicinal properties or used topically for skin and wound care.
